title: Agentic AI Developer
notes: Entry rung of the Agentic track and on-ramp to agentic-ai-engineer (L30). Teaches the LLM-application fundamentals the higher agentic tracks assume as prerequisites (LLM APIs, prompt engineering, structured output, tool/function calling, retrieval basics, deploying a simple LLM app). Maps to the high-volume 'AI Engineer / LLM application developer' hiring title. Seeded from role research 2026-06-15; broaden on first nightly research cycle.

modules.title: LLM Fundamentals for Application Developers
objectives: Call LLM APIs and reason about tokens, context windows, temperature, and model selection
Understand chat/messages format, system vs. user/assistant roles, and streaming
Estimate cost and latency of LLM calls
Handle API errors, rate limits, and retries robustly

modules.title: Prompt Engineering & Structured Output
objectives: Apply core prompting techniques (instructions, few-shot, role, decomposition)
Get reliable structured output (JSON / schema-constrained) from models
Validate and parse model output at the application boundary
Iterate on prompts with simple evals

modules.title: Tool & Function Calling
objectives: Define tools/functions and let the model choose and call them
Wire Python functions as tools and handle the call/result loop
Reason about when a tool call is appropriate vs. plain generation
Handle tool errors and malformed arguments safely

modules.title: Retrieval Basics (Embeddings & Simple RAG)
objectives: Generate embeddings and run similarity search
Build a simple retrieval-augmented generation pipeline
Chunk and index documents for retrieval
Evaluate retrieval quality at a basic level

modules.title: Your First Agent: The Reason-Act Loop
objectives: Understand and implement a basic ReAct (Thought/Action/Observation) loop
Combine tools + retrieval into a single simple agent
Add basic memory across turns
Recognize the limits of a single agent (handoff to the Engineer track)

modules.title: Shipping an LLM Application
objectives: Wrap an LLM app behind a simple API (FastAPI) with input validation
Manage API keys/secrets via environment variables
Add basic logging and a cost/usage guardrail
Containerize and run the app locally

projects.title: Capstone: Ship an LLM-Powered Application
projects.description: Build and deploy a small LLM-powered application (e.g., a domain assistant or document Q&A) combining prompting, structured output, tool calling, and simple RAG behind a validated API with secrets management and a usage guardrail.

projects.title: A Simple Tool-Using Agent
projects.description: Build a single agent with a ReAct loop, 2-3 tools, retrieval, and basic conversation memory. Document where it breaks down — the bridge into the Agentic AI Engineer track.
